name: Roast Beef Hash
recipeIngredient: 1 lb Roast beef, cut into 1/4-inch dice
3/4 lb Potatoes, boiled until tender and cut into 1/4-inch dice
1 Green bell pepper, seeded and finely chopped
Salt and freshly ground black pepper to taste
recipeInstructions:
1. In a mixing bowl, stir together the roast beef, potatoes, onions, green bell pepper, heavy cream, parsley, salt, and pepper until thoroughly blended.
2. Heat 2 tablespoons of the unsalted butter and 1 tablespoon of the vegetable oil in a 10-inch heavy skillet over moderately high heat.
3. Add the hash mixture to the skillet and use a spatula or the back of a wooden spoon to pack it firmly into the pan, on top and around its sides, to form a neat, solid cake.
4. Reduce the heat to low and cook the hash, occasionally shaking the skillet to prevent sticking, for 8 to 10 minutes until the underside is crusty and well browned.
5. Unmold the hash cake onto a large dinner plate by firmly holding the plate over the skillet and turning both the skillet and plate together.
6. Increase the heat and add the remaining butter and oil to the skillet. Slide the flipped hash cake back into the pan, reduce the heat, and cook for an additional 8 to 10 minutes.
7. Makes 4 servings.
"Breakfast & Brunch Book". From the Hayward Daily Review, 11/9/88.
Posted by Stephen Ceideberg; November 1 1992.
